text:
There was a little old oak tree in the backyard of a cottage nestled among whispering pines.

It was wise old Oakley. He had seen civilizations rise and fall. He'd watched children giggle as they tossed silt from their playdough castles into his gnarled branches.

One autumn afternoon, a young girl named Lucy approached Oakley with a basket laden with berries. "You are the guardian of these berries," she declared, her eyes shining. "I collect them all year."

Oakley listened as she sat on his broad, leafy hip. She told him stories of winters spent in knit knit hoodies by firelight. Oakley listened patiently, his branches swaying.

As the stars began to dot the velvet night sky, Lucy plucked a ripe blackberry. "I've saved them for you," she said solemnly. "They're special."

She placed the berry on his bark and left hurriedly.

The next morning, Oakley felt lighter.
